title: Information Ecosystem Flow
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Trace how environmental claims flow from scientific sources through media and algorithms, identifying where distortion occurs at each stage.

educational.topic: Information Ecosystem and Media Literacy for Environmental Claims
learningObjectives: Trace how environmental claims flow from original sources through media, algorithms, and social sharing
Identify specific distortion mechanisms at each stage of the information pipeline
Compare the accuracy of claims that travel through different pathways
Apply the information ecosystem model to evaluate real-world environmental claims
prerequisites: Basic understanding of scientific research and peer review
Familiarity with how social media algorithms work
standards: NGSS HS-LS2-7 Science and Engineering Practices

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Pipeline visualization with critical evaluation of real-world claims
keyQuestions: At which stage of the information pipeline does the most distortion typically occur?
Why do environmental claims that pass through more intermediaries tend to become less accurate?
How do platform algorithms amplify certain versions of environmental claims?
What steps can you take to trace an environmental claim back to its original source?
Why is it important to evaluate the pathway of information rather than just labeling it true or false?
commonMisconceptions: Students often believe information is either completely true or completely false, rather than distorted by degrees at each stage
Many students think algorithms are neutral filters rather than engagement-optimizing systems that can amplify sensationalized content
Students may assume that all published sources have equal credibility
assessmentOpportunities: Evaluate student distortion logs for completeness and accuracy of mechanism identification
Assess student-created information evaluation checklists for practical utility
Have students trace a real environmental claim and present their pathway analysis to the class
